question: Is the general office environment (walls, furniture, floor tiles) rendered in a photorealistic style?
Is the landscape painting hanging on the wall rendered in a traditional Chinese ink wash style?
Is the cat rendered in a traditional Chinese ink wash style (fluid brushstrokes, monochrome ink) rather than photorealistic fur?
Does the cat appear to be leaping out of the framed landscape painting (medium nesting/painting-within-painting effect)?
Are there distinct ink footprints visible on the photorealistic oak desk where the cat steps?
Is the mechanical arm lifting the coffee cup rendered in a Pop Art style with bold black outlines?
Is the reflection on the coffee surface rendered as Pop Art style color blocks rather than realistic reflections?
Is one building in the city skyline transforming into an Art Deco style geometric tower?
Is the female office worker rendered in a photorealistic style (distinct from her shadow)?
Is the worker's shadow rendered as a distorted, Expressionist style black silhouette trailing on the floor?
Are the multiple styles (Photorealism, Ink Wash, Pop Art, Art Deco, Expressionism) clearly distinguishable from one another without unintended blending?
Is the female office worker free from severe structural distortions (e.g., facial collapse, extra limbs) that are not attributed to the Expressionist shadow style?
